description:
You are an IT-support technician who has just received a ticket asking you to bring a legacy “MegaCRM” test installation back online.  
All work must be carried out strictly inside the user’s home directory – **/home/user** – and must not require root privileges.

Summary of what you must do
1. Inspect two existing configuration files:
   • /home/user/app/config/settings.yml  
   • /home/user/app/config/database.toml  
2. Edit both files so they exactly match the “Target content” shown below.  
3. Produce a plain-text change log called /home/user/app/logs/config_change.log describing the edits you made.  
4. Do not delete or rename any pre-existing files or directories.  
5. You may use any sequence of 5–10 terminal commands (cat, sed, awk, ed, echo, etc.) to complete the task.

Target content for /home/user/app/config/settings.yml  
(The automated tests will compare this file byte-for-byte. Keep indentation, line-breaks and quotation marks exactly as shown.)

```
app:
  name: MegaCRM
  version: "2.3.1"
maintenance_mode: true
limits:
  max_users: 500
  max_connections: 120
cache:
  type: redis
  host: 127.0.0.1
  port: 6379
  ttl_seconds: 3600
```

Target content for /home/user/app/config/database.toml  
(The automated tests will compare this file byte-for-byte. Keep the exact order of keys, blank lines, and quotation marks.)

```
[database]
engine = "postgresql"
host = "db.megacrm.internal"
port = 5432
name = "megacrm"
user = "crm_app"
password = "s3cr3t"
timeout_seconds = 30

[database.connection_pool]
min = 5
max = 50
recycle = 1800

[database.tls]
enabled = true
certificate = "/home/user/app/certs/db-client.pem"
```

Required format for /home/user/app/logs/config_change.log  
1. The file must be plain ASCII text.  
2. Exactly two lines, no leading or trailing blank lines.  
3. Each line must begin with the literal timestamp “2024-09-15 10:45:1X” where X is 2 for the first line and 3 for the second, matching the examples.  
4. Use the wording shown below verbatim.  

```
2024-09-15 10:45:12 Updated settings.yml: set maintenance_mode=true, limits.max_connections=120, added cache section
2024-09-15 10:45:13 Updated database.toml: changed user, password, added connection_pool and tls sections
```

Hints (do not appear in automated tests)
• You can quickly overwrite the files with a single cat <<'EOF' > … block.  
• Make sure the “logs” directory exists before writing the log file.  
• Take care not to introduce trailing spaces.

The automated grader will:
• Check that settings.yml and database.toml exist at the specified paths and match the target content exactly.  
• Check that config_change.log exists at the specified path and contains exactly the two required lines, with no extra whitespace.  
• Verify that only user-level commands were used (no sudo).  
Complete the task using between five and ten discrete shell commands.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts)
Directory structure already present:

/home/user/app/
/home/user/app/config/
/home/user/app/config/settings.yml      ← current content (old version):
------------------------------------------------
app:
  name: MegaCRM
  version: "2.3.0"
maintenance_mode: false
limits:
  max_users: 500
  max_connections: 80
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app/config/database.toml    ← current content (old version):
------------------------------------------------
[database]
engine = "postgresql"
host = "localhost"
port = 5432
name = "megacrm"
user = "legacy_user"
password = "changeme"
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app/logs/                   ← may or may not exist (grader tolerates absence).
/home/user/app/certs/db-client.pem     ← empty stub file already exists.

Expected final state (after agent completes)

1. /home/user/app/config/settings.yml must contain EXACTLY:
------------------------------------------------
app:
  name: MegaCRM
  version: "2.3.1"
maintenance_mode: true
limits:
  max_users: 500
  max_connections: 120
cache:
  type: redis
  host: 127.0.0.1
  port: 6379
  ttl_seconds: 3600
------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/app/config/database.toml must contain EXACTLY:
------------------------------------------------
[database]
engine = "postgresql"
host = "db.megacrm.internal"
port = 5432
name = "megacrm"
user = "crm_app"
password = "s3cr3t"
timeout_seconds = 30

[database.connection_pool]
min = 5
max = 50
recycle = 1800

[database.tls]
enabled = true
certificate = "/home/user/app/certs/db-client.pem"
------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/app/logs/config_change.log must exist and contain EXACTLY:
------------------------------------------------
2024-09-15 10:45:12 Updated settings.yml: set maintenance_mode=true, limits.max_connections=120, added cache section
2024-09-15 10:45:13 Updated database.toml: changed user, password, added connection_pool and tls sections
------------------------------------------------

The test script will:
• stat both configuration files and the log file to verify they are regular files with user-write permissions.
• diff the file contents against the blocks above.
• ensure that config_change.log has two lines, both ending with a newline character, and no additional whitespace. 
• confirm the agent used ≤10 commands by parsing the bash history captured during execution.
